raw:
Let me check the actual file paths for frontend files.
Now let me check the frontend API file path in the file-sizes.csv to understand types:
```json
{"score": 3, "explanation": "Backend has clear layers (policy/ pure, services/ business+db, routes/ HTTP) with all business constants centralized in policy/ and imported without duplication. No cycles, no orphans, no files >300 lines in production code. Domain errors used consistently. Frontend splits pages/, components/, api.ts. However: (1) routes import from db directly (loan-handler.ts line 2: `import { getDb } from '../db.js'`) — a layer leak; (2) services call raw `db.prepare()` with no thin DB-access abstraction; (3) frontend organized by technical role not by feature; (4) no shared types boundary between frontend/backend; (5) tests aggregated in a single 493-line file rather than per-service. These issues prevent level 4, but the core architecture is sound, well above the level 2 threshold of duplicated business constants or DB rows leaking to HTTP responses."}
```

parsed.explanation: Backend has clear layers (policy/ pure, services/ business+db, routes/ HTTP) with all business constants centralized in policy/ and imported without duplication. No cycles, no orphans, no files >300 lines in production code. Domain errors used consistently. Frontend splits pages/, components/, api.ts. However: (1) routes import from db directly (loan-handler.ts line 2: `import { getDb } from '../db.js'`) — a layer leak; (2) services call raw `db.prepare()` with no thin DB-access abstraction; (3) frontend organized by technical role not by feature; (4) no shared types boundary between frontend/backend; (5) tests aggregated in a single 493-line file rather than per-service. These issues prevent level 4, but the core architecture is sound, well above the level 2 threshold of duplicated business constants or DB rows leaking to HTTP responses.
